right guidance (object of تُعَلِّمَنِ - teach me)
indeed you (subject of لَن تَسْتَطِيعَ - will not be able)
will never (negates تَسْتَطِيعَ - you will be able)
with me (referring to Moses speaking to Khidr)
patience (object of تَسْتَطِيعَ - you will be able to)
And how? (asking about تَصْبِرُ - you being patient)
upon/over (indicating position or authority over مَا لَمْ تُحِطْ - what you have not encompassed)
you/she encompass (in negative context of لَمْ)
knowledge/information (object of تُحِطْ - you did not encompass)
you will find me (speaking to Musa)
patient (object of سَتَجِدُنِي - you will find me)
and not (connected to أَعْصِى - I will disobey)
for you (connected to سَتَجِدُنِىٓ - you will find me)
then do not (connected to اتَّبَعْتَنِى - if you follow me)
it was brought up (as a topic)
a mention (object of أُحْدِثَ - I relate)
and so they both set out
they both rode (referring to Moses and Al-Khidr boarding the ship)
in (location preposition for السَّفِينَةِ - the ship)
he made a hole in it (acted on)
he said (introducing his response to خَرَقَهَا/he made a hole in it)
did you make a hole in it
certainly indeed (emphasizing جِئْتَ - you have brought)
a thing (object of جِئْتَ - you have brought)
you are able (after لَن meaning 'will not' in this ayah)
patience (object of 'you will not be able to have')
you hold me responsible (connected to لَا to mean 'do not hold me responsible')
with what (referring to نَسِيتُ - I forgot)
I forgot (explaining to موسى why he made a mistake)
you make difficult for me (Khidr speaking to Musa)
any hardship (as the object being acted upon in تُرْهِقْنِى)
and so they both set forth
until (connecting to لَقِيَا - they both met)
when (followed by لَقِيَا - they both met)
they both met (referring to Moses and Al-Khidr in the story)
a boy (object of لَقِيَا - they both met)
and then he killed him (the boy they met)
a soul/person (object of قَتَلْتَ - you killed)
a thing (object of قَتَلْتَ - you killed)
something very wrong or terrible (as a direct object)
indeed you (addressing Moses in this ayah)
an excuse (object of بَلَغْتَ - you have reached)
and so the two of them proceeded
so they both found (after they came to the town)
a wall (object of وَجَدَا they found)
he wants/seeks (wall that wants to fall)
it was about to collapse (of جِدَارًا - a wall)
if you had (showing something that didn't happen in the past)
you would have taken for yourself
upon him/it (referring to أَجْرًا - a payment)
you are able (in a conditional phrase with لَمْ)
as for (introduces topic that السَّفِينَةُ explains)
they work/act/do (referring to the poor people who own the boat)
that (connects أَرَدتُّ 'I wanted' to أَعِيبَهَا 'damage it')
I damage it (referring to السَّفِينَةُ - the ship)
and he was (describing مَّلِكٌ - a king)
behind them (referring to the poor people who owned the boat)
a king (who takes ships by force)
he takes/is taking (subject of مَّلِكٌ - a king)
and as for (contrasts with what came before)
the young boy (subject of كَانَ - was)
and so he was (result of what happened in previous ayah)
two believers (describing the parents in أَبَوَاهُ)
so we felt fear (leads to أَن يُرْهِقَهُمَا - that he would burden them)
that (connects to يُرْهِقَهُمَا - he would burden them)
into rebellion (object of يُرْهِقَهُمَا - burden them)
and in disbelief (connected to طُغْيٰنًا - transgression)
that (connects to يُبْدِلَهُمَا - He would substitute them)
he exchanges/will exchange them both (referring to the parents exchanging their son)
something better (object of يُبْدِلَهُمَا - replace them with)
and nearer/closer (in kindness than the previous son)
for/belonging to two boys (owned by الجِدَارُ)
under/beneath it (referring to الْجِدَارُ - the wall)
surely for both of them (refers to the two orphan boys)
father of them both (referring to the two orphan boys)
and so he wanted (connecting to previous events in the story)
their full strength/maturity (referring to the two orphan boys)
I did it (referring to the previous actions mentioned in the story)
what (referring to the actions previously mentioned)
patience (object of تَسْطِع - you could bear)
and they ask you (about ذِى ٱلْقَرْنَيْنِ)
possessor of (connected to الْقَرْنَيْنِ - the two horns)
the two horns (possessed by ذِى/owner of)
say! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in response to their question)
upon you all (related to سَأَتْلُوا۟ - I will recite)
for him (object of مَكَّنَّا - we established)
from (shows where something starts or what it's made of)
a means (object of آتَيْنٰهُ/We gave him)
a path/means (as the object being followed)
he reached (subject of وَجَدَ - he found)
the sun (owned by مَغْرِبَ - setting place)
he found it/her (referring to الشَّمْسِ - the sun)
at/near it (referring to الشَّمْسِ - the sun)
a people (object of وَجَدَ - he found)
either/whether (introducing options in أَن تُعَذِّبَ 'to punish' and أَن تَتَّخِذَ 'to adopt')
that (introduces the choice 'either to punish' in إِمَّآ أَن تُعَذِّبَ)
in them (referring to the people Dhul-Qarnayn found)
with goodness (describing how something will be done)
he wronged (subject of نُعَذِّبُهُ - we will punish him)
then soon (before نُعَذِّبُهُ - we will punish him)
a punishment (object of يُعَذِّبُ - he will punish)
a terrible punishment (object of فَيُعَذِّبُهُۥ عَذَابًا)
And as for (introduces a new topic)
and he worked/did (subject of فَلَهُۥ - then for him)
then for him (connected to جَزَآءً - reward)
a reward (object of فَلَهُ - for him is)
the best outcome (reward for good deeds)
ease (as the object of سَنَقُولُ - we will speak)
then/next (connecting أَتْبَعَ to what came before)
a way/means (object of أَتْبَعَ - he followed)
until (shows the end point of something)
he reached (مَطْلِعَ ٱلشَّمْسِ - the rising place of the sun)
the sun (owned by مَطْلِعَ - rising place)
he found her/it (referring to الشَّمْسِ - the sun)
we make/set (in response to لَّمْ before it)
below/beneath it (referring to الشَّمْسِ - the sun)
a cover (object of وَجَدَهَا - he found it)
with him, near him (what was known about in خُبْرًا)
then, next, afterward (connects to أَتْبَعَ - he followed)
a path/means (object of أَتْبَعَ - he followed)
until, up to (shows a limit or endpoint)
when (showing a future or conditional time)
he reached (connected to حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا - until when)
between (acted on by بَلَغَ - he reached)
below/beneath them both (referring to السَّدَّيْنِ/the two barriers)
they understand deeply (referring to the people mentioned as قَوْمًا in the ayah)
a saying (object of يَفْقَهُونَ - they understand)
the two horns/ages (part of the title ذا القرنين)
those who spread corruption (subject of يَأْجُوجَ وَمَأْجُوجَ)
so would you? (asking for permission from ذَا ٱلْقَرْنَيْنِ)
a payment (object of نَجْعَلُ - we make)
that (introduces what they want يَأْجُوجَ وَمَأْجُوجَ to do)
you make/set (in response to هَلْ نَجْعَلُ - shall we make)
in it (referring to what Dhul-Qarnayn was enabled with)
my lord (subject of قَالَ - he said)
better (than what Dhul-Qarnayn's people can offer)
between you all (referring to Dhul-Qarnayn's helpers)
and between them (object of أَجْعَلْ - I will make)
a barrier (object of أَجْعَلْ - I will make)
when/if (connecting to سَاوَىٰ - he made equal)
when (introduces the time condition for جَعَلَهُۥ - he made it)
he made it (referring to the iron blocks) into something
upon it/him (referring to the molten copper being poured)
molten copper (object of أُفْرِغْ - I may pour)
then what/so what (introduces consequence in relation to يَظْهَرُوهُ)
that/to (connects to يَظْهَرُوهُ - they could scale/climb it)
and they were not (able to ٱسْتَطٰعُوا۟)
they were able to (subject of يَظْهَرُوهُ - to climb over it)
and when (connects to جَعَلَهُ - he made it)
promise (subject of came in فَإِذَا جَآءَ)
he made it (referring to the barrier/wall)
completely flattened/leveled (describing the wall in the story)
and he was (connected to جَعَلَهُ meaning 'he made it')
truly/really (describes how something is done in وَعْدُ رَبِّى حَقًّا)
and it was blown (into الصُّورِ - the Horn)
in the horn, the trumpet (blown into in وَنُفِخَ فِى)
a gathering (as the object being described)
Hell (the object being shown in عَرَضْنَا)
for the rejecters of truth (object of عَرَضْنَا)
as a display (emphasizing how we displayed Hell)
a cover (being used with the word عَن meaning 'from')
and they were (those whose eyes were covered from My remembrance)
they are able to (referring to those whose eyes were covered)
hearing (as the object being affected in لَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ)
do they then think? (questioning the thinking of الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟)
besides me/other than me (connected to عِبَادِى meaning 'My servants')
Hell (as the object of أَعْتَدْنَا - we have prepared)
as hospitality (what is prepared by اللَّهِ)
about the ones who are greatest in loss (object of نُنَبِّئُكُم)
deeds/actions (as the thing being described in أَخْسَرِينَ/greatest losers)
those who (subject of ضَلَّ - went astray)
he went completely astray (describes الَّذِينَ - those who)
they do well (thinking they are doing good deeds)
those (pointing to something far) (subject of كَفَرُوا)
those who/the ones who (subject of كَفَرُوا)
they rejected/denied (referring to those who rejected in أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ)
by/with the signs (of رَبِّهِمْ - their Lord)
and so they became worthless (referring to أَعْمٰلُهُمْ - their deeds)
any weight (being acted upon by نُقِيمُ - we establish/give)
that (pointing to something far) (subject of جَزَآؤُهُمْ)
those who (referring to a group of people who ءَامَنُوا۟)
and they did (referring to ٱلَّذِينَ - those who)
of the highest garden of Paradise
as a hospitality gift (describing جَنَّاتُ ٱلْفِرْدَوْسِ)
those who remain (plural, in accusative case)
they seek/desire (referring to the dwellers of Paradise)
away from it/her (referring to جَنَّاتِ - gardens)
a change/alternative (object of يَبْغُونَ - they seek)
he/it was (describing the sea/ocean in this ayah)
ink (as the supply/resource for كَلِمٰتِ رَبِّى - the words of my Lord)
it would run out/become depleted (before كَلِمٰتُ)
words (subject of تَنفَدَ - to run out)
with its like (referring to the sea mentioned before)
as additional support/aid (object of جِئْنَا/we brought)
only/just (restricting what follows in أَنَا۠ بَشَرٌ)
a human being (predicate of إِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠)
to me (connected to يُوحَىٰٓ - is revealed)
that only/merely (emphasizes limitation in the statement 'I am only a human')
your (plural) god (the one you all worship)
One (as a complete description of Allah)
then let him do/perform (in response to hoping to meet his Lord)
a righteous work (object of فَلْيَعْمَلْ - let him do)
he associates partners (present tense, in context of لا - meaning 'not')
